name: skills/conversational_skills/capture_activity_details.md
applicability: 5-section memory skill for capturing activity details (type, location, participants, temporal). Directly applicable to JASON-OS memory (T4). This is a worked example of 'skills about how to remember' — the exact concept for your multi-layer memory research.
home_connection: Memory skill format for JASON-OS. Template for memory operation instructions.

name: skills/conversational_skills/insert.md
applicability: Memory skill for inserting new facts. Includes duplicate avoidance, fact splitting, temporal capture. The constraints ('skip trivial, fleeting, or speculative content') encode memory quality criteria.
home_connection: Your auto-memory system could use similar quality criteria for what to save.

name: skills/conversational_skills/capture_temporal_context.md
applicability: Memory skill for temporal details. Your auto-memory converts relative dates to absolute dates — this skill formalizes that pattern as a reusable instruction.
home_connection: MEMORY.md temporal handling. Already doing this ad hoc.

name: 15 skill files (8 conversational + 7 embodied)
applicability: The complete skill bank output. 15 evolved memory skills in 5-section format. Each is a reusable instruction for how to process memory operations. Study the full set for JASON-OS memory architecture.
home_connection: Multi-layer memory research (T4, T16). 15 worked examples.

name: arXiv 2602.02474 — MemSkill paper
applicability: Core theory behind the framework. Explains skill evolution loop, failure mining, dual-embedding memory bank. NOT FETCHED this session — flagged for future deep-research.
home_connection: Theoretical foundation for JASON-OS memory. Must read before T4 execution.

name: Meta-memory framework
applicability: Skills about HOW to remember, not what. 'What kinds of memory to extract, how to remember, where to focus, what to preserve or forget.' This reframes your auto-memory from 'save facts' to 'learn HOW to save facts.'
home_connection: Paradigm shift for auto-memory design. Currently rule-based; could be skill-based.

name: Skill evolution loop
applicability: Mine challenging examples → analyze failures → refine existing skills + propose new ones → repeat. A self-improving system. Applicable to any skill that should get better over time.
home_connection: Meta-skill: could your skill-audit evolve its own evaluation criteria?
